text.title: Django Quality Toolchain Rules
text.summary: Ruff, mypy+django-stubs, bandit, and pre-commit setup rules for a deterministic Django quality baseline.

section.title: Ruff Replaces Black+isort+flake8

rule.statement: Use ruff for both formatting and linting. Do not install black, isort, or flake8 alongside ruff — they overlap and add CI time.
rule.rationale: ruff is 10-100x faster than the three-tool stack, covers all the same rules plus more (DJ, UP, SIM, T20), and is configured in one place (pyproject.toml).

rule.statement: Enable rule groups: E, F, I, B, UP, SIM, DJ, T20, PT, RUF, S. Exclude migrations via extend-exclude. Ignore E501 (let formatter wrap) and S101 (allow assert in tests).
rule.rationale: These groups cover formatting, pyflakes errors, import order, bugbear, upgrade hints, Django-specific, no-print, pytest, and ruff-native rules.

section.reference: Full ruff + mypy config block for Django

rule.statement: Install mypy and django-stubs. Set django_settings_module in [tool.django-stubs], not only as an environment variable. Without it, CI fails when DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE is not in the shell.
rule.rationale: Agents commonly set DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE as env var only. The [tool.django-stubs] setting is required for mypy to find the Django config.

rule.statement: Enable mypy strictness per module via [[tool.mypy.overrides]], not repo-wide. Never flip strict = true for an entire legacy repo in one commit.
rule.rationale: Repo-wide strict mypy on a legacy codebase produces hundreds of errors. Module-by-module ratcheting is the only sustainable path.

rule.statement: Every # type: ignore must include the specific error code and a comment explaining why. Block PRs with blanket # type: ignore suppressions.
rule.rationale: Blanket suppressions hide real type errors and accumulate silently. Specific codes are self-documenting and reviewable.

section.title: Pre-commit Hooks

rule.statement: Wire pre-commit with ruff, ruff-format, bandit, end-of-file-fixer, trailing-whitespace, and check-yaml hooks. Run pre-commit run --all-files once at adoption and commit the format pass alone before feature work.
rule.rationale: A standalone format commit keeps history readable — subsequent feature commits contain only logic changes.

rule.statement: Run python manage.py check --deploy in production-facing CI. It catches DEBUG, ALLOWED_HOSTS, and SECURE_* misconfigurations that linters cannot see.
rule.rationale: Django's built-in deployment check covers settings that are valid in development but dangerous in production.

section.reference: Modern pre-commit config with ruff and bandit

list.item: Install ruff, mypy, django-stubs, bandit, pre-commit
Write .pre-commit-config.yaml with ruff, ruff-format, bandit, pre-commit-hooks
Run pre-commit install and pre-commit run --all-files
Commit format-only pass before first feature commit
Add pre-commit run --all-files step to CI
Run pip-audit weekly via scheduled GHA job
